failedAssertions.reason: Graph replay throughput is LOWER than C dispatch baseline: C graph dispatch=35.2 tok/s, Python graph dispatch=36.2 tok/s vs C dispatch=37.8 tok/s. Graph is ~7% slower. The overhead from 512 hipGraphExecKernelNodeSetParams calls per token (for mutable param updates: cos/sin, seq_len, KV cache ptrs) exceeds the savings from replacing ~960 hipModuleLaunchKernel calls with hipGraphLaunch calls. On gfx906/ROCm 7.1, hipGraphLaunch with per-step param updates does not outperform the C dispatch tight loop.

testScriptsRun.result: PARTIAL — segfault during graph correctness test (re-capture after disable/re-enable stale C plan issue). Key assertions VAL-GRAPHDECODE-001, 003, 004 confirmed PASS before crash.
PARTIAL — compile/load PASS, single-GPU PASS (22.2 tok/s), correctness PASS (min cosine_sim=0.996823), throughput FAIL (35.2 tok/s < 37.8 tok/s)

discoveredIssues.description: test_graph_decode.py segfaults when graph dispatch is disabled then re-enabled: _c_graph_dispatch_plan is NOT reset in set_graph_dispatch(False), causing the old destroyed graph exec handles to be reused on the next capture. The C graph dispatch plan is built after capture and cached, but not invalidated when the underlying graphs are destroyed.
discoveredIssues.workaround: Use a fresh engine instance per graph dispatch test, or fix set_graph_dispatch(False) to also reset _c_graph_dispatch_plan.

discoveredIssues.description: VAL-GRAPHDECODE-002 fails: hipGraphExecKernelNodeSetParams overhead (512 calls/token) dominates over graph replay savings on gfx906/ROCm 7.1. The mutable param update approach adds more overhead than it saves.
discoveredIssues.suggestedFix: Consider pre-updating all mutable params via device-side parameter buffers (GPU-side update of cos/sin offsets) to avoid host-side hipGraphExecKernelNodeSetParams overhead per token.
